SEARCH
Package minum.web

Class WebFramework

java.lang.Object
minum.web.WebFramework
public class WebFramework extends Object
Responsible for the logistics after socket connection

Relies on server sockets built in WebEngine. Builds a function in makePrimaryHttpHandler() that sort-of fits into a slot in WebEngine and handles HTTP protocol. Also handles routing and static files.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    WebFramework(Context context)
    This constructor is used for the real production system
    WebFramework(Context context, ZonedDateTime overrideForDateTime)
    This provides the ZonedDateTime as a parameter so we can set the current date (for testing purposes)

  • Constructor Details

    • WebFramework

      public WebFramework(Context context)
      This constructor is used for the real production system

    • WebFramework

      public WebFramework(Context context, ZonedDateTime overrideForDateTime)
      This provides the ZonedDateTime as a parameter so we can set the current date (for testing purposes)
      Parameters:
      overrideForDateTime - for those test cases where we need to control the time

  • Method Details

    • isThereIsABody

      public boolean isThereIsABody(Headers hi)
      Determine whether the headers in this HTTP message indicate that a body is available to read

    • makePrimaryHttpHandler

      public ThrowingConsumer<ISocketWrapper,IOException> makePrimaryHttpHandler()
      This is the brains of how the server responds to web clients. Whatever code lives here will be inserted into a slot within the server code This builds a handler Consumer that provides the code to be run in the web testing engine that selects which function to run for a particular HTTP request. See makePrimaryHttpHandler(Function)

    • registerPath

      public void registerPath(StartLine.Verb verb, String pathName, Function<Request,Response> webHandler)
      Add a new handler in the web application for a combination of a StartLine.Verb, a path, and then provide the code to handle a request.
      Note that the path text expected is *after* the first forward slash, so for example with http://foo.com/mypath, you provide us "mypath" here.

    • registerStaticFiles

      public void registerStaticFiles(StaticFilesCache sfc)

    • getDb

      public <T extends DbData<?>> Db<T> getDb(String name, T instance)
      Since this is a generic method, a bit of care is required when calling. Try to use a pattern like this: 
       Db<Photograph> photoDb = wf.getDb("photos");
